prompt: The functional interface has too many abstract methods and cannot be used with a lambda. What is the bug?
choices: Lambda expressions cannot be assigned to interface types
Converter has two abstract methods; a functional interface must have exactly one abstract method for lambda assignment — Converter is not a functional interface
int return types are not supported in functional interfaces
The @FunctionalInterface annotation must be present for lambda assignment
explanation: A functional interface must have exactly one abstract method (SAM — Single Abstract Method). Converter has two abstract methods (convert and reverseConvert), so it's not a functional interface and cannot be implemented with a lambda. Fix: remove reverseConvert or move it to a separate interface. Add @FunctionalInterface to catch this at compile time.
